author: codex-root

patch.value: Enforce erasable zero-escape TypeScript and complete source docstrings

Remove explicit any and dynamic imports, inline trivial one-use helpers, verify external API types from installed packages, prohibit non-erasable syntax, and require high-quality documentation coverage for every source file and public contract.

No explicit any or dynamic import remains in src; erasable-only TypeScript rules are mandatory; every source file and exported API has a meaningful JSDoc contract; automated lint/doc coverage gates report 100%; no trivial one-call helper remains
Source pattern inventory and installed dependency types reviewed
Makes implementation intent and type contracts inspectable by maintainers and agents
Prevents unsafe escape hatches and undocumented behavior from entering production
Strict, fully documented, erasable TypeScript across the source tree
The current source contains explicit any and seven dynamic import sites and has no enforced doc coverage

message: Track mandatory TypeScript and docstring ratchet

author: codex-root
message: Begin strict TypeScript and docstring work

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Initial source inventory found explicit any in src/utils/exit.ts and src/api/system.ts plus dynamic imports in cli-config, extended, settings-wizard, services, and lists. Installed Undici types will be used for the multipart body instead of a guessed cast.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: 2026-07-21 strict scan: bun typecheck passes with erasableSyntaxOnly; source and test scans now contain no explicit any casts/types or dynamic import expressions after replacing formatter fixtures with exact HA types and the fs mock with a static module mock.

value.note: Strict erasable TypeScript lint docstrings duplication file-size and shell gate

message: Link strict source quality acceptance gate

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Erasable TypeScript enforcement is most reliable as a lint contract paired with installed-package type inspection; branch coverage also exposed redundant defaults whose removal simplified runtime semantics.

patch.value: Enabled zero-explicit-any, no dynamic or inline imports, erasable-only syntax, and a deterministic 115/115 source docstring gate while removing trivial redundant branches.
All source is type-safe, erasable, documented, lint-clean, and free of trivial one-use wrappers introduced by this work.
Typecheck and ESLint pass; docstring coverage is 115/115; all source remains under 300 code lines; full behavioral coverage is 100%.
Strict erasable type safety and complete source-file documentation are enforced and locally green.

author: codex-root
message: Release completed type quality item claim
